qwen3.6-35b-a3b|think.justification: Best overall response: gets all three sail colors right, correctly counts 3 steamships on horizon, identifies the horse riders, mentions surfboard and beach ball, gets beach hut colors right. Avoids the pier/carriage/Dick Bruna hallucinations. Outstanding precision with good recall. The benchmark-setter for this image.

notes: Dense Waldo scene rewards models that identify specific micro-elements (sail colors, horses, steamship count, pyramid, volleyball) and severely penalizes canonical hallucinations (hovercraft-as-pier, carriage-pulled-by-horses, Dick Bruna attribution). Top performers: qwen3.6-35b-a3b|think (9.0) nails all three sail colors, 3 steamships, and horses without hallucinating; qwen3.5-9b|think (8.5) and qwen3.6-27b|think (8.5) similarly strong. Notable that the think variants of larger Qwen models outperform their nothink counterparts here (35b: 9.0 vs 7.5, 27b: 8.5 vs 7.0, 9b: 8.5 vs 4.5) suggesting thinking helps with detail enumeration in dense scenes. qwen3-vl-4b|think failed catastrophically with repetition loop. gemma4-31b|think got all 3 sail colors but committed the pier hallucination elaborately. qwen3.5-4b|nothink uniquely caught horses/ice-cream-carts/picnic-baskets but fatally hallucinated Dick Bruna as artist. Deterministic scores in input were often overgenerous (e.g., 9.2 for several models that missed key details) — holistic scores are typically 1-3 points lower due to missed micro-elements and hallucinations the deterministic probes don't catch.
